mn13554c-deehju.title: Design todo REST API
mn13554c-deehju.description: Design a REST API for a todo-list. CRUD: create, list, get, update, delete. Todo fields: id (UUID), title (string), completed (boolean), createdAt (ISO 8601). Provide endpoints, methods, request/response JSON, error codes.
mn13554c-deehju.result:
# Todo List REST API — Design

## Base URL

```
/api/v1/todos
```

---

## Endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `POST` | `/todos` | Create a todo |
| `GET` | `/todos` | List all todos |
| `GET` | `/todos/{id}` | Get a single todo |
| `PATCH` | `/todos/{id}` | Update a todo |
| `DELETE` | `/todos/{id}` | Delete a todo |

**Why PATCH over PUT?** — Clients should only send the fields they want to change. A full PUT replacement is brittle and wasteful for partial updates.

---

## Data Model

| Field | Type | Read-only | Notes |
|-------|------|-----------|-------|
| `id` | `string` (UUID v4) | ✅ | Server-generated |
| `title` | `string` | ❌ | 1–255 chars, required on create |
| `completed` | `boolean` | ❌ | Defaults to `false` |
| `createdAt` | `string` (ISO 8601) | ✅ | Server-generated |

---

## Endpoints in Detail

### `POST /todos` — Create

**Request:**
```json
{
  "title": "Buy groceries"
}
```

**Response `201 Created`:**
```json
{
  "id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
  "title": "Buy groceries",
  "completed": false,
  "createdAt": "2026-03-22T09:35:00.000Z"
}
```

---

### `GET /todos` — List

**Query Parameters (all optional):**

| Param | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `completed` | `boolean` | — | Filter by status |
| `limit` | `integer` | `50` | Max items (1–100) |
| `offset` | `integer` | `0` | Pagination offset |

**Response `200 OK`:**
```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
      "title": "Buy groceries",
      "completed": false,
      "createdAt": "2026-03-22T09:35:00.000Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "f9e8d7c6-b5a4-3210-fedc-ba0987654321",
      "title": "Ship feature",
      "completed": true,
      "createdAt": "2026-03-21T14:00:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "total": 42,
    "limit": 50,
    "offset": 0
  }
}
```

**Why `meta.total`?** — Clients need the total count to build proper pagination UIs (e.g., "Page 3 of 9").

---

### `GET /todos/{id}` — Get

**Response `200 OK`:**
```json
{
  "id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
  "title": "Buy groceries",
  "completed": false,
  "createdAt": "2026-03-22T09:35:00.000Z"
}
```

---

### `PATCH /todos/{id}` — Update

All fields optional. Send only what changes.

**Request (mark complete):**
```json
{
  "completed": true
}
```

**Request (rename):**
```json
{
  "title": "Buy groceries and snacks"
}
```

**Response `200 OK`:**
```json
{
  "id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
  "title": "Buy groceries and snacks",
  "completed": true,
  "createdAt": "2026-03-22T09:35:00.000Z"
}
```

> No `updatedAt` field by design — the spec only called for four fields. If audit trails matter later, add it. Don't over-engineer day one.

---

### `DELETE /todos/{id}` — Delete

**Response `204 No Content`**

Empty body. `204` means "I did it, nothing to return." Clean.

---

## Error Responses

All errors follow a consistent envelope:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
    "message": "Title is required and must be 1-255 characters."
  }
}
```

| HTTP Status | Error Code | When |
|-------------|------------|------|
| `400` | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Missing/invalid fields, bad query params |
| `404` | `NOT_FOUND` | Todo `{id}` doesn't exist |
| `409` | `CONFLICT` | UUID collision (theoretical) |
| `422` | `UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY` | Valid JSON but semantically wrong (e.g. `completed: "maybe"`) |
| `429` | `RATE_LIMITED` | Too many requests |

**5xx errors** are internal — never expose stack traces. Return:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "INTERNAL_ERROR",
    "message": "Something went wrong."
  }
}
```

---

## Design Decisions & Rationale

| Decision | Why |
|----------|-----|
| **Plural noun resource** `/todos` | REST convention. No verbs in URLs. |
| **UUID v4** | No ID enumeration attacks. Client never guesses IDs. |
| **Envelope with `data` + `meta` on list** | Separates payload from pagination. Room to grow (links, cursors). |
| **Flat envelope on single item** | One item doesn't need `data`/`meta` wrapping — it's the resource itself. |
| **`PATCH` not `PUT`** | Partial updates. Reduces payload size and race-condition surface. |
| **`204` on delete** | Standard. No body to parse. |
| **Error envelope with `code` + `message`** | Machine-parseable `code`, human-readable `message`. Client can switch on codes. |
| **No auth in v1** | Keep it simple. Add `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` middleware when needed. |

---

## Next Steps (when you're ready)

- **Auth** — JWT middleware, scoped to user. Add `userId` as a filter.
- **Soft delete** — `deletedAt` timestamp instead of hard delete.
- **Cursor pagination** — Replace `offset`/`limit` with opaque cursors for stable pagination at scale.
- **OpenAPI/Swagger spec** — Auto-generate docs from this design.
- **Etags / `If-Match`** — Optimistic concurrency control for updates.

Want me to turn this into an OpenAPI 3.1 spec, scaffold a working implementation (Express/FastAPI/Go), or wire up the database schema?

mn1364e6-u1loj2.title: Implement todo API server
mn1364e6-u1loj2.description: Implement a simple todo-list REST API server in Python using only stdlib (http.server). Endpoints: POST /todos (create), GET /todos (list), GET /todos/<id> (get), PATCH /todos/<id> (update), DELETE /todos/<id> (delete). Store todos in memory. Return JSON responses. Handle 404 for missing todos.
mn1364e6-u1loj2.result:
All green. Here's what's in `todo_server.py`:

| Method | Endpoint | Description | Status |
|--------|----------|-------------|--------|
| `POST` | `/todos` | Create a todo (JSON body) | 201 |
| `GET` | `/todos` | List all todos | 200 |
| `GET` | `/todos/<id>` | Get one todo | 200 / 404 |
| `PATCH` | `/todos/<id>` | Partial update (JSON body) | 200 / 404 |
| `DELETE` | `/todos/<id>` | Delete a todo | 200 / 404 |

**Key details:**
- Pure stdlib — `http.server` + `json`, no deps
- In-memory dict store with auto-incrementing IDs
- JSON in/out on all endpoints
- `id` field is immutable (PATCH can't overwrite it)
- Unknown routes and methods return 404/405
- Runs on `0.0.0.0:8000` — `python3 todo_server.py`

Want me to persist it to disk, add filtering/query params, or anything else?

mn137bej-debv79.title: QA review todo API design
mn137bej-debv79.description: Review the todo REST API design and implementation. Check: 1) All CRUD operations defined 2) Proper HTTP status codes 3) Error handling complete 4) JSON schemas consistent 5) Edge cases covered. Provide a pass/fail assessment with specific issues found.
mn137bej-debv79.result:
I can't find any todo REST API code to review. Here's what I checked:

- **Workspace files** — only the default OpenClaw bootstrap/config files, no project source code.
- **Memory** — no prior context about a todo API project.
- **Team status** — no team tasks assigned.

**To proceed, I need one of these:**

1. **Paste the code** — drop the API implementation (routes, handlers, models, schemas) into chat and I'll review it line by line.
2. **Point me to a repo** — give me a GitHub URL or file path and I'll clone/fetch it.
3. **Check your project location** — if the code lives somewhere else on the system, tell me the path and I'll read it directly.

Which works for you?
